text.title: strict-mode-required-fields Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for strict-mode-required-fields. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: For every object schema submitted with strict: true, set additionalProperties: false, and list every declared property in required. To express "this field may be absent", use a nullable type ({"type": ["string", "null"]} or Pydantic str | None) — never omit the field from required.
rule.rationale: The strict-mode compiler builds a regular grammar; optional fields would introduce branch non-determinism (skip token vs read token), and unrestricted additional properties would force the grammar to accept arbitrary tokens between every key. The OpenAI error 400: Invalid schema for response_format ... additionalProperties must be false is the compiler refusing the schema.

rule.statement: Encode "may be absent" by declaring the field as T | None = None and keeping it in required. Never drop a property from required to mean "optional" under strict mode.
rule.rationale: Strict-mode compilers translate T | None into {"type": ["T","null"]} and keep the property required, which is the only encoding the FSM accepts.

rule.statement: Before deploying a strict-mode pipeline, dump Model.model_json_schema() and assert that for every nested object: (a) additionalProperties is false, (b) the required list equals the properties keys.
rule.rationale: The strict-mode 400 error fires at request time, not build time; an automated assertion catches the regression before traffic hits.
